headline: NBC Reloads Primetime With Four Bold Series Orders
lede: NBC is staging a dramatic comeback with a quartet of high-profile series orders that signal a robust programming strategy for 2026-27.
body:
### Drama Renaissance Leads Network's Pilot Season

NBC is making a decisive bet on procedural drama and star-driven comedy with four series pickups that promise to reinvigorate its primetime lineup. The network's drama slate looks particularly strong, with rebooted IP and veteran actors anchoring two potential hit series.

### Star Power Drives New Programming

David Boreanaz headlines the revived "Rockford Files" remake, bringing his considerable network drama experience to a classic detective franchise. Simultaneously, Peter Krause returns to network television with "Line of Fire", suggesting NBC is betting on familiar faces to draw audiences.

On the comedy front, Jake Johnson leads the single-camera "Sunset P.I.", potentially offering a fresh comedic take on law enforcement tropes. The multi-camera "Newlyweds" reunites television veterans Téa Leoni and Tim Daly, promising chemistry-driven situational humor.

### Strategic Programming Move

This aggressive slate represents NBC's most ambitious pilot season since the pandemic disrupted traditional television development. By mixing established actors, familiar franchise concepts, and varied comedy formats, the network appears to be hedging its bets across multiple audience demographics.
key_facts: Four series ordered across drama and comedy genres
David Boreanaz and Peter Krause lead drama pickups
Jake Johnson and Téa Leoni headline comedies
Marks most robust pilot season since COVID-19 pandemic
